details.text: Derivative: الْعُسْرَىٰ is the superlative form of عَسِير and literally means the hardest or most difficult. الْعُسْرَىٰ can imply the path towards evil that will be made easy and facilitated for the person.
Derivative: تَرَدَّىٰ means to fall or tumble down. It is used for someone following from a mountain, from its top to its bottom. It is said to refer to falling into hellfire.

إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا لَلْهُدَىٰ

- “No doubt, the guidance is (exclusively) incumbent upon Us”. by placing عَلِيْنا (upon us) before لَلْهُدَىٰ (the guidance) in This sentence structure, it implies exclusivity i.e., the provision of guidance is exclusively mandated upon Us.

وَإِنَّ لَنَا لَلْآخِرَةَ وَالْأُولَىٰ

- “No doubt, the hereafter and the first (life) belong to Us”. By placing لَنَا ((belongs) to us) before لَلْآخِرَةَ وَالْأُولَىٰ (the hereafter and the first (life)) in this sentence structure, it implies exclusivity i.e., the hereafter and the first (life) exclusively belongs to Us.
- الْأُولَىٰ “The first (life)” is used when the attribution is to Allah and not دُنْيا (the life of this world) which is used in connection to Allah’s servants. That is because دُنْيا implies that which is lower, less and inferior.
Derivative: أَنْذَرْتُ “I have warned” is from أَنْذَرَ – يُنْذِرُ – إِنْذَار which means to warn and encompasses explaining the danger of a matter, warning about it and then rousing fear about it.
Derivative: تَلَظَّىٰ is originally تَتَلَظَّى but has the original تاء (Taa’ – ت) omitted. It refers to a fire that keeps blazing and igniting itself as if fuel continues to be poured into it.
Derivative: الْأَتْقَى – Is the one who has “the most Taqwa” i.e., consciousness and fear of Allah.
Derivative: يُجَنَّبُ means to be far removed from جَنَّبَ – تَجِنِيب which means to keep away from something. This is the passive form and means to be kept away or to be averted.
Derivative: تَجَلَّىٰ means to appear, reveal and manifest itself.
Derivative: وَجْهِ means pleasure and contentment.
Derivative: ابْتِغَاءَ means “to seek and desire something” and “for the purpose of attaining or achieving something”.

وَمَا خَلَقَ الذَّكَرَ وَالْأُنْثَىٰ

- The مَا (Maa) here can be ما المَوْصُولَة and carrying the meaning of “the One who”. In which case the verse would mean: “By (or I swear by) the (incredible) One who created the male and the female”. The مَا (Maa) could also be مَا المَصْدَرِيَة in which case the focus would be upon the creation itself and not the Creator, to imply: “I swear by how incredibly awe-inspiring the creation of the male and the female is”.

Derivative: شَتَّىٰ means varied and diverse meaning not the same and nor in harmony or conformity. شَتَّىٰ in origin refers to something that in origin was one or the same and then was broken apart, became scattered and dispersed and as a result became diversified. شَتَّىٰ is the plural of شَتِيت.

Derivative: سَعْيَ means to pace fast; slower than running and faster than walking. Such fast pacing is usually related to accomplishing something important or urgent. From this understanding سَعْيَ also carries the meanings of efforts and endeavours i.e., those things that you strive to achieve and its verb سَعَى can mean “to strive”.

Derivative: اتَّقَىٰ in origin means to ward or protect yourself from an evil (or harm) and its consequences. It refers to undertaking precautionary actions out of fear of falling into harmful consequences.
Derivative: أَعْطَىٰ means to give and implies to give in abundance.
Derivative: أَمَّا means “As for” and it is a conditional statement that requires an answer or response to complete it i.e., “As for the one who does such and such, then such and such will happen”.

Derivative: الْحُسْنَىٰ (Husnaa) is the feminine form of أَحْسَن (Ahsan) and is in the اِسْمُ التَّفْضِيل (Ism ut-Tafdeel – Superlative and comparative adjective) form. الْحُسْنَىٰ refers to that which is the best or ultimate good. الْحُسْنَىٰ has been said to refer to لا إله إلا الله “There is no Ilaha (deity worthy of worship and obedience) other than Allah” and all that which it encompasses and dictates i.e., to have Iman in everything that is obligatory to have Iman in and to practically follow its dictates. It has also been said to refer to Jannah (paradise).

Derivative: صَدَّقَ means to affirm and testify to the truth of something. It can carry the meaning of believing in something. صَدَّقَ – تَصْديق not only means to affirm but also implies that it is acted upon.

Derivative: اليُسْرَىٰ (Al-Yusraa) is the feminine of أَيْسَر which means “The easiest”. It comes from يَسِير (Yaseer) which means “easy” and implies something that can be done without falling into difficulty. اليُسْرَى can be understood to refer all the doors being opened for someone for goodness and righteousness or implies the path towards goodness and righteousness that will be made easy and facilitated for the person.
Derivative: اسْتَغْنَىٰ – to feel to be not in need of anyone or to consider oneself free of need and self-sufficed.
